findings.evidence: `rg` shows only origin-specific declarations at `FunctionClosure.lean:99`, `:118`, `:138`, `:161`; their bodies all reduce each summand with `rw [hF0 i hi]; simp`, so the distinguished zero is not part of the argument.
findings.fix: Prove a general pointwise lemma such as `sum_smul_apply_of_apply_eq_one {x : N} (hFx : ∀ i ∈ s, F i x = 1)` with no `[Zero N]`, derive the `apply_zero` lemmas by specializing `x := 0`, and derive multiplication/real-weight variants from the smul/coercion versions.
findings.issue: The normalized-mixture value lemmas are under-generalized to `0` and require `[Zero N]`, although their proofs only use evaluating all functions at the same point; lines 99, 118, 138, and 161 then duplicate the same `Finset.sum_congr` argument for smul/mul and complex/real weights.

summary: The closure API is at the existing positive-definite-function level, but the normalization lemmas are introduced only as origin-specific duplicated algebraic special cases. Factor the point-evaluation result first and derive the origin and notation variants from it.
